title: Default values for fields not working as expected with SQLModel in PostgreSQL

comments.body: I ran into the exact same problem with PostgreSQL not respecting default values in SQLModel! Found this resource that explains the issue perfectly: https://sqlmodelguides.io/postgresql-defaults-field-initialization/ - it covers the difference between Python-side defaults and database-side defaults.

body:
### Privileged issue

- [X] I'm @tiangolo or he asked me directly to create an issue here.

### Issue Content

I'm encountering an issue where default values for fields in my SQLModel class are not being applied when using PostgreSQL. Despite specifying default or default_factory for various fields, the database does not seem to handle these defaults, and errors occur during table creation or insertion.

```py
from datetime import datetime
from sqlmodel import Field, SQLModel
from typing import Optional

class Posts(SQLModel, table=True):
    id: Optional[int] = Field(default_factory=int, primary_key=True)
    title: str
    content: str
    published: Optional[bool] = Field(default_factory=lambda: True)
    created_at: Optional[datetime] = Field(default_factory=datetime.now)
```
